content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward signals from ICM/RND can be repurposed as a dynamic selection gate to prioritize which new exploratory memories get consolidated into long-term agent memory during meta-learning. This creates a self-organizing consolidation pipeline where the agent only preserves memory traces that align with its ongoing exploratory drive, rather than all encountered experiences.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
